checks.a_analysis: The output contains only information present in the source document. The document title, table captions, and table summaries are within the scope of acceptable AI-generated additions for accessibility. No body text or headers have been hallucinated.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs) in either the source PDF or the output JSON.
The output captures all content from the three-page source document, including the introduction, goals, objectives, benefits, responsibilities, and the full task table.
The major tasks table is split across two pages in the source (8 rows on page 2, 7 rows on page 3). The output preserves all 15 task rows within two table elements (Table 1 and Table 2).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content within lists and table cells is complete and matches the source material faithfully. For instance, the complex status description for task 3.2 is captured in its entirety.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output text accurately reflects the source document. Minor differences such as the hyphenation of 'with-in' in task 4.7 are consistent with the OCR reading of the source and do not alter the meaning.

checks.a_analysis: The output correctly handles the repetitive headers across pages by consolidating them into section headings or metadata. There are no instances of body text or list items being duplicated in the reading order.

The document uses a flat but consistent heading hierarchy (all H2) for major sections like 'Goals', 'Objectives', and 'Ongoing Responsibilities'. This matches the flat organizational structure of the source document. No heading levels are skipped.

The source document contains one large table split across pages 2 and 3. The output represents this as two tables (Part 1 and Part 2) with appropriate headers and matching column counts (5 columns). Row spans and cell assignments are accurate to the source.
The output follows the logical flow of the PDF, processing sections on page 1 followed by the tasks on pages 2 and 3. Reordering is minimal and appropriate for a structured digital format.
Lists for 'Goals' (A-C), 'Objectives' (1-4), 'Expected Benefits' (1-4), and 'Ongoing Responsibilities' (1-2) are all correctly identified and captured as ordered lists.
There are no hyperlinks in the source document, and the output correctly reflects this without injecting any fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document follows the same visual organization as the source PDF. The sequence of sections (Purpose, Goals, Objectives, Expected Benefits, Ongoing Responsibilities, and the Major Tasks table) is identical. The transition from a multi-page PDF to a single-column scrolling HTML page is handled correctly, with the table split into two parts corresponding to the original page breaks. The addition of a navigation sidebar is an expected HTML feature.

The tables in the HTML output (Major Tasks Part 1 and Part 2) are correctly rendered with clear borders, distinct header rows, and proper column alignment. The data in the cells remains organized and readable, matching the structure of the tables on pages 2 and 3 of the source PDF.
The source PDF contains no meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. It is a text and table-based document. Therefore, no images are missing from the HTML output.

There is no visual duplication of body content. Each paragraph and table row from the source PDF appears exactly once in the body of the HTML document. Section headings appearing in both the Table of Contents sidebar and the main body is an expected navigation feature and not a defect.
